name: Parque Nacional de Los Picos de Europa – circular from Riaño
name_original: Parque Nacional de Los Picos de Europa – circular desde Riaño
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Embalse de Riaño are set within the Cantabrian Mountains, offering diverse terrain for cyclists. The region features the Embalse de Riaño reservoir, surrounded by peaks and limestone formations, with parts of the area lying within the Picos de Europa National Park. Cyclists encounter varied landscapes, including Atlantic forests with oak and beech trees, and scenic valleys. The mountainous environment provides both challenging ascents and panoramic views. Best road cycling routes around Embalse de Riaño The most popular road cycling route is Parque Nacional de Los Picos de Europa – circular from Riaño, a 44.8 miles (72.1 km) trail that takes 3 hours 50 minutes to complete. This route offers a circular journey through the Picos de Europa National Park. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is San Glorio Pass – Historic center of Potes loop from Riaño, a difficult 71.3 miles (114.7 km) path. This route includes significant elevation gain as it loops towards San Glorio Pass and Potes. Local road cyclists also love the Puerto de Picones (1316 m) – Velilla del Río Carrión loop from Riaño, a 66.4 miles (106.8 km) trail leading through mountainous terrain, often completed in about 5 hours 3 minutes. Road cycling around Embalse de Riaño is defined by mountainous terrain, reservoir views, and limestone formations. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate to difficult. The routes in Embalse de Riaño are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 3.9 stars from over 10 reviews. More than 800 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Embalse de Riaño's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Embalse de Riaño?
A: Komoot features 35 road cycling routes around Embalse de Riaño. These routes offer a variety of experiences, from moderate to difficult, allowing cyclists to explore the region's diverse landscapes.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes in Embalse de Riaño?
A: Road cycling around Embalse de Riaño is characterized by mountainous terrain with significant elevation changes, offering dramatic vistas of the reservoir and the Cantabrian Mountains. You'll encounter well-paved roads winding through Atlantic forests, past limestone formations, and along scenic valleys, with some routes potentially including unpaved segments.

Q: Are there any easy road cycling routes suitable for beginners or those seeking a relaxed ride?
A: While many routes in Embalse de Riaño are moderate to difficult, there is at least one easy road cycling route available on komoot. The region generally offers a range of options, including routes with modest elevation gains, making it possible to find a less challenging ride.

Q: What are some notable landmarks or viewpoints I can see while cycling around Embalse de Riaño?
A: The region is rich in natural and cultural landmarks. You can cycle past the impressive Riaño Dam, enjoy remarkable views from the 'Most Beautiful Bench in León' near the Ermita de Nuestra Señora del Rosario, or experience the thrill of Spain's Biggest Swing at the Mirador de las Hazas. Other points of interest include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5443521" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hórreo of Salio&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6811232" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Enchanted Pine Forest with Wooden Masks&lt;/a&gt;. Many routes also offer stunning &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6308896" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;views of the Riaño Reservoir&lt;/a&gt; itself.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes in the area?
A: Yes, Embalse de Riaño offers several circular road cycling routes. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1042280997/parque-nacional-de-los-picos-de-europa-circular-from-riano" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Parque Nacional de Los Picos de Europa – circular from Riaño&lt;/a&gt;, which takes you through the national park. Another challenging loop is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38167384" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerto de Picones (1316 m) – Velilla del Río Carrión loop from Riaño&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Embalse de Riaño?
A: The summer months, particularly June and August, see a high number of cyclists in the region. The weather during these times is generally favorable for exploring the mountainous terrain. However, the dramatic landscapes are beautiful year-round, with spring and autumn offering cooler temperatures and vibrant scenery.

Q: Is it possible to rent bikes in Embalse de Riaño?
A: Yes, electric bike rentals are available in the area. This provides an accessible way for visitors to explore the mountainous terrain, even if they don't bring their own equipment or prefer assistance on the climbs.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy most about road cycling in Embalse de Riaño?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 3.9 stars. Reviewers often praise the stunning panoramic views, the challenging mountainous terrain, and the diverse natural features, including the tranquil reservoir and the peaks of the Cantabrian Mountains.

Q: Are there any road cycling routes that pass through the Picos de Europa National Park?
A: Yes, parts of the Embalse de Riaño region lie within the Picos de Europa National Park, offering routes that traverse this stunning natural area. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1042280997/parque-nacional-de-los-picos-de-europa-circular-from-riano" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Parque Nacional de Los Picos de Europa – circular from Riaño&lt;/a&gt; is a prime example, providing a circular journey through the park's impressive landscapes.

Q: Are there any long-distance or advanced road cycling routes for experienced riders?
A: Experienced road cyclists will find several challenging routes with significant elevation gains. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14991455" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Glorio Pass – Historic center of Potes loop from Riaño&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 71.3-mile (114.7 km) path with substantial climbing. Another demanding option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24635356" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bridge to Riaño – View of the Riaño Reservoir loop from Carande&lt;/a&gt;, which covers over 125 km.

Q: Where can I find parking for road cycling routes around Embalse de Riaño?
A: The town of Riaño serves as an ideal starting point for exploring the area and typically offers parking facilities. Many routes also begin from other villages or designated areas around the reservoir, where parking may be available. It's advisable to check specific route details for recommended starting points and parking options.

Q: Are there cafes or places to stop for refreshments along the road cycling routes?
A: The town of Riaño and other villages in the vicinity offer amenities such as cafes and restaurants where cyclists can stop for refreshments. While specific cafes on every route segment are not guaranteed, planning your ride to pass through these towns will provide opportunities for breaks and meals.
tips: Impressive views of Picos de Europa and the Valley of Santa Marina de Valdeón. There are interpretive panels and a car park from where you can start your route.
Spectacular view of the Picos de Europa mountain range. One of the most famous national parks. Natural border between the communities of Castilla y León and Asturias.

Riaño, known as the "new Riaño," rises next to the reservoir that bears its name, surrounded by spectacular mountains in the heart of the León mountains. It is a picturesque and strategic location for exploring the surrounding peaks and valleys, as well as the Picos de Europa.

waypoints.name: View of the Picos de Europa
View of Riaño and the Reservoir
